name: I slipped and fell at work in Salt Lake City but didn't tell anyone right away, what should I do now?
description: Report immediately to your employer despite delay—Utah gives 180 days max, but sooner avoids denial; document details and seek medical care explaining work link

mainEntity.name: I slipped and fell at work in Salt Lake City but didn't tell anyone right away, what should I do now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report immediately to your employer despite delay—Utah gives 180 days max, but sooner avoids denial; document details and seek medical care explaining work link.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Report the injury to your employer immediately, even if delayed, as Utah law allows up to 180 days but prompt reporting preserves benefits.[1] Employers must notify their insurer within 7 days, and you'll receive forms like the First Report of Injury (Form 122).[1] Key factors: Document how, when, and where it happened; get medical attention and explain the work connection to the doctor.[1] See your doctor for a Physician’s Initial Report (Form 123); request copies of all forms; avoid signing anything without understanding.[1] Consulting a workers' comp specialist can protect your rights if issues arise.[1]
